mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies deployment complexity and operational overhead by providing a standardized framework for agent communication. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential resource overhead.

steps.title: Install the Kagenti CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the Kagenti platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This installs the CLI on a Linux system.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.4.0-alpha.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region as necessary.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to access the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the Kubernetes cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install Kagenti on the cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys Kagenti to the Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --create-namespace --version 0.4.0-alpha.1

```

> Add the Kagenti Helm repository.
> This installs Kagenti in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-installation verification
steps.description:
This step verifies that the Kagenti deployment is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Check that all pods are in 'Running' state.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster incurs costs based on the resources used.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti deployment from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> This command removes the Kagenti release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step upgrades Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kagenti -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n 1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --version 0.4.0-alpha.1

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Drain the pods before upgrading.
> This command upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kagenti -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -n 1 kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating: pods "kagenti-..." is forbidden: error looking up service account kagenti/kagenti: serviceaccount "kagenti" not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service account for Kagenti was not created during installation.

**Fix:**
Create the service account using: kubectl create serviceaccount kagenti -n kagenti

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded' when trying to access the Kagenti UI
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kagenti service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the pods with: kubectl get pods -n kagenti and troubleshoot any non-running pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Kagenti" in version "v1"
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) for Kagenti has not been installed.

**Fix:**
Ensure the CRDs are installed with: helm install kagenti/kagenti --set installCRDs=true

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to connect to the server' when running kubectl commands
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-run the kubeconfig setup command: gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

versionNotes.changes: Initial release with support for AI agent deployment and orchestration.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
